provenance.source_corpus: Recovery in a multiple stressor environment: using the reference condition approach to examine zooplankton community change along opposing gradients

nodes.name: reductions in the atmospheric deposition of sulphur and metal particulates

source_spans.text: Following major reductions in the atmospheric deposition of sulphur and metal particulates, chemical and biological recovery have been observed in many lakes
Following major reductions in the atmospheric deposition of sulphur and metal particulates, chemical and biological recovery have been observed in many lakes
While increases in pH have led to an overall recovery of zooplankton across a broad geographic scale
While increases in pH have led to an overall recovery of zooplankton across a broad geographic scale
other stressors including metal contamination and eutrophication in urban environments may greatly affect recovery processes and outcomes
other stressors including metal contamination and eutrophication in urban environments may greatly affect recovery processes and outcomes
Among the acidiﬁed lakes, most lakes with pH ,5.5 contained species poor copepod communities
Among the acidiﬁed lakes, most lakes with pH ,5.5 contained species poor copepod communities
suggesting that the delays in cladoceran recovery we found were caused by increased urban stresses
suggesting that the delays in cladoceran recovery we found were caused by increased urban stresses
It appears that the recovery of zooplankton communities cannot necessarily be expected until the return of the pre-acidiﬁcation predator community has occurred
It appears that the recovery of zooplankton communities cannot necessarily be expected until the return of the pre-acidiﬁcation predator community has occurred
Copepod recovery may have been limited by residual metal toxicity
Copepod recovery may have been limited by residual metal toxicity
This was most likely a result of elevated metal toxicity, which appears to exclude both piscivorous fish and cyclopoid copepods from establishing in these lakes
This was most likely a result of elevated metal toxicity, which appears to exclude both piscivorous fish and cyclopoid copepods from establishing in these lakes
This was most likely a result of elevated metal toxicity, which appears to exclude both piscivorous fish and cyclopoid copepods from establishing in these lakes
The most important variable was alkalinity, which alone explained 15% of the variation
All 18 physical and chemical variables had significant marginal effects with the abundance of cladocerans
Cyclopoid copepods such as D. b. thomasi and M. edax were strongly, negatively correlated with pH
Cyclopoid copepods such as D. b. thomasi and M. edax were strongly, negatively correlated with pH
Urban lakes with impaired copepod communities had elevated metal levels as well as low concentrations of DOC and ions, which can increase metal toxicity through increased bioavailability
Urban lakes with impaired copepod communities had elevated metal levels as well as low concentrations of DOC and ions, which can increase metal toxicity through increased bioavailability
Valois et al. (Valois et al., 2010) attributed the dominance of H. gibberum in some of these lakes to the ability of this species to out-compete D. mendotae at high levels of planktivory
Valois et al. (Valois et al., 2010) attributed the dominance of H. gibberum in some of these lakes to the ability of this species to out-compete D. mendotae at high levels of planktivory
